content.body:
Conductor track: `foi_o_v2_empirical_implementation_20260714`

Define and export the versioned FOI-O ontology, schema, codebook, provenance, and candidate-status contract needed for a reproducible re-extraction of a pinned `fyi-archive-nz` snapshot.

Architecture boundary:
- `fyi-cli` owns capture from FYI/Alaveteli.
- `fyi-archive` owns immutable manifests and derived-layer publication.
- `nlp-policy-nz` owns ontology-pinned NER/NLP execution.
- `fe-reader` is deferred and is not a dependency.

Acceptance:
- contract revisions are pin-able and machine-readable;
- raw source labels and evidence remain preserved;
- outputs remain candidate-only until human review;
- re-extraction produces a delta against the initial ontology-based baseline;
- no raw archive record is overwritten.

Related tracks:
- `edithatogo/nlp-policy-nz:foio_archive_adapter_20260714`
- `edithatogo/fyi-archive:foio_derived_reextraction_20260714`
content.title: Track: FOI-O V2 extraction contract and governed archive re-extraction

Conductor track: `au_foi_corpus_readiness_20260714`

Produce pinned, rights-aware, jurisdiction-stratified public request examples for FOI-O validation, beginning with an operator-approved Commonwealth + NSW pilot.

Acceptance includes raw-state and correspondence preservation, attachments/WARC/WACZ completeness, rights and takedown records, outcome coverage reporting, jurisdiction isolation, remote digest verification, and explicit insufficiency reports. `fyi-cli` remains the only capture implementation. NLP candidates remain a separate derived layer.

content.body:
Conductor track: `foio_au_jurisdiction_adapter_20260714`

Extend the FOI-O archive adapter with profile-isolated Australian extraction. Pilot Commonwealth and NSW, then add the other seven jurisdictions only after FOI-O profile and empirical gates pass.

Acceptance includes pinned archive/legal/ontology/model revisions, jurisdiction routing and abstention, cross-profile contamination tests, candidate-only output, and per-jurisdiction precision, recall, F1, calibration, coverage, disagreement, and abstention reporting.
content.title: Track: FOI-O Australian jurisdiction extraction adapter

items.title: Track: FOI-O Australian jurisdiction extraction adapter

content.body:
Conductor track: `foi_programme_governance_20260714`

Use GitHub Project 14 as the focused FOI programme board across `foi-o`,
`fyi-cli`, `fyi-archive`, `nlp-policy-nz`, `rac-conformance`, FOI-specific
`legislation` work, and Alaveteli workflow/state intelligence.

Keep repository-native Conductor plans authoritative, retain dedicated Project
membership, mirror only FOI-relevant items from multi-purpose repositories, and
add drift checks for missing, stale, or unrelated Project 14 items.

## FOI-O to PIC pairing

Define an optional, versioned compatibility profile. FOI-O remains authoritative
for its runtime, schemas, ontology, codebook, and jurisdiction profiles; PIC is
an interchange and conformance layer and is not a FOI-O runtime dependency.

The release handshake must pin:

- FOI-O release/tag/SHA, capabilities, schemas, ontology, codebook, and migrations;
- PIC package versions and compatibility results;
- jurisdiction profile, calendar, applicable time, and observation time;
- `edithatogo/legislation` source-pack revision and digest;
- immutable `fyi-archive` raw provenance and Hugging Face derived-dataset
  repository/revision/configuration/split/digest;
- `nlp-policy-nz` pipeline/model version for derived candidates; and
- evidence assertions, independent oracle, curator/reviewer, and promotion state.

Preserve FOI-O epistemic, review, extraction, maturity, and certification axes;
do not collapse them into PIC `valueState`. Add offline validation, valid and
negative examples, a compatibility matrix, and cross-jurisdiction/time/digest
leakage tests. Generated mappings, fixtures, parameters, and traces remain
candidates until independently reviewed.

Detailed recommendations:
`conductor/tracks/foi_programme_governance_20260714/foio-pic-integration.md`.
content.title: Track: Freedom of Information programme governance

content.body:
Parent paper programme: #24

Trigger only after edithatogo/foi-o publishes the next immutable release and release-evidence bundle.

Required evidence:
- release tag and commit SHA;
- contract/schema versions and capability matrix;
- migration and reproducibility commands;
- tests, fixtures, provenance, empirical results, exception summary, and limitations.

Then diff the evidence against manuscript claims, regenerate affected figures/tables/citations, run paper-artifact QA, and prepare the human submission approval packet.

Cross-repo provenance: edithatogo/foi-o#27 and conductor/tracks/foi_o_v2_empirical_implementation_20260714/.

## Current status (2026-07-15)

FOI-O v0.8.1 is now published at commit d24ae6f9f2d9488052969f633d91eff4a9a47f58, and its citation and Zenodo evidence is recorded. The immutable-release trigger is therefore partially satisfied, but the required release-evidence bundle remains pending in edithatogo/foi-o issue 27. No paper refresh or submission is authorized until that bundle is available and reviewed.
content.title: Gate: Refresh papers after next foi-o release

items.title: Gate: Refresh papers after next foi-o release

content.body:
## Summary

- complete the FOI quality, citation, Project 14 drift, and machine-readable release-evidence work
- wrap PIC fixtures, parameters, and traces in a jurisdictional/bitemporal evidence envelope
- add content-addressed offline compatibility validation and fail-closed independent-oracle promotion
- provide a one-pass human verification packet while preserving the absent FOI-O V2 release as an explicit blocker

## Issues

Progresses #30, #31, and #33. These issues remain open where publication, release, or human verification is still required.

## Validation

- `make check`
- 11 tools tests
- 46 contract tests, 87.20% coverage
- 25 converter tests
- 20 harness tests
- 33 SNAP tests
- 16 NZ reconciliation tests
- 6 service-boundary tests
- 2 Docassemble tests
- live Project 14 allowlist: 19 observed / 19 required

## Remaining external gates

- publish FOI-O V2 and its immutable release-evidence bundle
- Dylan verifies the prepared Project 14/boundary packet and independently reviews any candidate promotion
- Zenodo publication/DOI verification and manuscript authorization
- upstream maintainer workflow, signing, review, and merge actions
content.title: Complete repo-owned FOI programme blocker remediation

items.title: Complete repo-owned FOI programme blocker remediation

content.body:
## Evidence-backed gap

The archive and Australian FOI-O adapters are deterministic candidate-generation contracts, but their current fixtures use placeholder revisions/hashes and synthetic sources. The independent panel therefore rejects legal-label promotion from the current evidence.

## Scope
- pin real immutable FOI-O, fyi-archive, legislation/profile and model revisions
- build rights-cleared, jurisdiction-isolated held-out samples for NZ/archive, Australian Commonwealth and NSW
- record reviewer/adjudicator identities, dates, disagreements and promotion decisions
- publish per-family and per-jurisdiction precision, recall, F1, calibration, coverage and abstention reports
- keep every output candidate-only until its jurisdiction gate passes

## Acceptance
No promotion from placeholder or synthetic evidence. Each jurisdiction is independently reproducible and can abstain or remain unpromoted.
content.title: FOI-O empirical promotion evidence for NZ, Commonwealth and NSW

items.title: FOI-O empirical promotion evidence for NZ, Commonwealth and NSW
